headline: AI won’t save advertising, says Digitas’ Amy Lanzi
summary: Amy Lanzi, CEO of Digitas North America, states AI won't revolutionize advertising. She shared her perspective at the Cannes Lions festival. This reflects the industry's evolving view on AI's impact.

headline: Mystery box shows are complicated for everyone — even the actors
summary: The showrunner of Apple TV's 'Silo' admits the series' complexity leads to confusion. Even actors faced issues with plot details during filming. This highlights the challenges of producing intricate 'mystery box' narratives.

headline: BitTorrent’s disastrous, legendary, and controversial story
summary: BitTorrent marks 25 years since its creation by Bram Cohen. The platform has a legendary yet controversial history. It significantly impacted peer-to-peer file sharing.

headline: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
summary: OpenAI proposed giving the US government a 5% ownership stake. This aims to ease tensions with the Trump administration. It also seeks to address public backlash against AI.

headline: Even KFC is Dunking on Sony, as the Dust Settles on PlayStation's Shock Decision to Kill Physical Game Discs
summary: Sony announced it will stop releasing new PlayStation games on disc from 2028. The decision sparked widespread negative online feedback. Even major brands like KFC have commented on the move.

headline: Get 10 Months of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate for £73 in Today's Best UK Deals
summary: Users can secure 10 months of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate for £73. This deal utilizes an improved EA Play loophole. Other notable UK tech deals include Corsair, Sony, and Apple products.

headline: 'He Was on the Valium or Something': Matt Smith Can't Believe Keanu Reeves Was Spotted Watching the Infamously Terrible Movie Morbius on a Flight
summary: Matt Smith expressed disbelief over Keanu Reeves reportedly watching 'Morbius'. Reeves was seen viewing the infamously panned film on a flight. Smith himself starred in the superhero movie.

headline: Apple TV Teases Neuromancer Adaptation With an Iconic Line From William Gibson's Influential Cyberpunk Novel
summary: Apple TV is adapting William Gibson's seminal cyberpunk novel 'Neuromancer'. A 22-second clip teased the series with its iconic opening line. The show is set to be a 10-episode adaptation.

headline: Labour failed to prepare for power, admits PM's former top aide
summary: Morgan McSweeney, former top aide, admitted Labour failed to prepare for power. He stated the party did not deliver quickly enough in office. This revelation came in his first-ever interview.

headline: People smuggler convicted in France found by BBC living in UK and seeking asylum
summary: A man convicted of people smuggling in France was found living in the UK. The BBC located him working in a Leicestershire village. He is reportedly seeking asylum in the UK.

headline: Venezuela quake survivor pulled out alive after eight days
summary: Hernán Gil was rescued after eight days trapped under rubble in Venezuela. He was in a concrete hut when a multi-story car park collapsed. This marks a remarkable survival story.

headline: Forced adoptions 'a stain on our history' says Starmer as he gives formal apology
summary: Keir Starmer formally apologized for historical forced adoptions in the UK. He described the practice as 'a stain on our history'. Thousands of babies were taken from mothers between 1949 and 1976.
